title: SOLID and Clean Architecture
learningObjectives: Defend **SOLID and Clean Architecture** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why SOLID and Clean Architecture matters
sections.content: **SOLID** and **Clean** **Architecture** separate **stable** **business** rules from **volatile** **details**. Interviews reward **boundary** discipline and honest **boilerplate** cost talk.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - SOLID and Clean Architecture
sections.content:
### Day 77 — **SOLID** & **Clean** **Architecture**

### 1. SRP / cohesion
One **reason** to **change** per unit.

### 2. OCP / DIP
Depend on **abstractions** at **boundaries**.

### 3. Clean rings
**Entities**, **use** **cases**, **interface** **adapters**, **frameworks**.

### 4. Testing
**Fast** tests on **core** without **web** **containers**.

### 5. Failure domain
**Leaky** **framework** imports in **domain**.

### 6. Story
**God** **service** split into **use** **cases** + **ports**—**deploy** **risk** drops.

sections.title: Basic — SOLID acronym anchor
sections.output: S O L I D = coupling/cohesion guardrails
sections.description: Print SOLID letters as memory hook.

sections.title: Intermediate — Dependency direction
sections.output: dependencies point to policies not frameworks

sections.title: Advanced — Port vs adapter
Clean Architecture rings
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 77 — Rings
rectangle Frameworks
rectangle Adapters
rectangle UseCases
rectangle Entities
Frameworks -down-> Adapters
Adapters -down-> UseCases
UseCases -down-> Entities
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — SOLID and Clean Architecture (Day 77 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: multiple unrelated reasons to change in one class

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is **SRP** in one **interview** sentence?
conceptual.answer: One axis of change per module—mixed reasons to change signal a split candidate. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **SOLID and Clean Architecture**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. One axis of change per module—mixed reasons to change signal a split candidate. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. One axis of change per module—mixed reasons to change signal a split candidate. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

conceptual.question: **Dependency** **rule** in **Clean** **Architecture**?
conceptual.answer: Source dependencies point inward; frameworks and I/O live in outer rings. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **SOLID and Clean Architecture**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Source dependencies point inward; frameworks and I/O live in outer rings. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Source dependencies point inward; frameworks and I/O live in outer rings. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: **Open/Closed** without **speculation**?
conceptual.answer: Extend via interfaces and composition where change is likely—not abstract everything day one. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Term | Recall |
|---|---|
| SRP | One change axis |
| OCP | Extend behavior |
| LSP | Safe substitution |
| ISP | Small interfaces |
| DIP | Abstractions inward |
| Port | Domain need |
| Adapter | Tech impl |
| Use case | App service |
| Entity | Core rules |
| Anti | God service |
